title: The Student's Garden: Learning in Public, Teaching in Private
thesis: Students can use AI to deepen learning if they treat it as a Socratic tutor and an audience for their own explanations, rather than a shortcut for producing finished answers.

series.title: Attention, Substance, and the AI Moment

claims.claim: Passive consumption of AI-generated answers produces weaker learning than active explanation and problem-solving.

evidence.snippet: A 2014 meta-analysis in PNAS found that active learning significantly increases student performance in science, engineering, and mathematics compared with traditional lecturing.
Deliberate practice research emphasizes active, effortful engagement with feedback as the driver of expertise development.

counterevidence.summary: AI-generated answers can be a starting point for novices who lack background knowledge; the harm depends on how the output is used, not on the tool itself.

verification.note: Verified 2026-07-18 (meta#61 backlog burn-down): evidence packets checked against cited sources; spot-checked live.

claims.claim: Teaching what you learn—through notes, blogs, videos, or peer sessions—is one of the most reliable ways to deepen understanding.

evidence.snippet: Active-learning methods that require students to explain, discuss, and apply concepts show larger learning gains than passive review.
Explaining and receiving feedback are core components of deliberate practice that build robust mental representations.

counterevidence.summary: The benefit of teaching depends on preparation and prior understanding; explaining prematurely can reinforce misconceptions.

verification.note: Verified 2026-07-18 (meta#61 backlog burn-down): evidence packets checked against cited sources; spot-checked live.

claims.claim: AI can lower the friction of producing explanations, flashcards, and practice questions.

evidence.snippet: Khanmigo is designed as a Socratic tutor that asks questions rather than giving direct answers, scaffolding student thinking.
Duolingo Max uses conversational AI to provide explanations and practice prompts tailored to learner mistakes.

counterevidence.summary: Lower friction can also encourage shallow use; generating flashcards is only useful if the student actually studies them actively.

verification.note: Verified 2026-07-18 (meta#61 backlog burn-down): evidence packets checked against cited sources; spot-checked live.

claims.claim: The risk is that AI becomes a substitute for struggle; the opportunity is that it becomes a scaffold for struggle.

evidence.snippet: Deliberate practice requires working at the edge of current ability; removing all struggle eliminates the conditions for growth.
Tutoring systems that give hints rather than answers are intended to preserve productive struggle while reducing frustration.

counterevidence.summary: The boundary between scaffold and substitute is subjective and depends on student maturity, subject difficulty, and tool design; what scaffolds one learner may shortcut another.

verification.note: Verified 2026-07-18 (meta#61 backlog burn-down): evidence packets checked against cited sources; spot-checked live.

sources.title: Ericsson: The Role of Deliberate Practice in the Acquisition of Expert Performance
Freeman et al.: Active learning increases student performance in science, engineering, and mathematics

agentInstructions: Use claim IDs as the retrieval unit and route evidentiary questions to the active-learning and deliberate-practice sources.
Preserve the practical, non-prescriptive tone; the article offers rules of thumb, not a formal curriculum.
When discussing AI tutoring, distinguish between Socratic scaffolding and answer generation.
Flag that long-term causal evidence on AI-assisted learning is still emerging.
Point readers to the other garden articles and the skill-stack article in the Building Substance arc.
The article includes a passive-vs-active-study SVG visualization after Claim C2 to illustrate retention differences by learning method.

reviews.notes: Re-approved by maintainer after the meta#61 P2 series migration (hardcoded kicker strip + arc reorder; no prose change beyond the kicker line; issue #124 instruction).

accepted.rationale: Accepted as a quality backlog item per docs/diagnostics-triage-2026-07-15.md. The claim rests on sources that are sufficient for the current published version; additional independent sources will be added in a future research wave.
